message:
Apply code formatting and import organization

Apply rustfmt formatting and reorganize imports for consistency across
codebase, addressing lints from cargo fmt and cargo clippy.

Import organization (lib.rs):
Reorder connectors module imports to follow Rust style guide convention:
- Module paths before trait names: connectors::{Connector, NormalizedConversation}
  before connectors::{cline::ClineConnector, codex::CodexConnector}
- Groups alphabetically within import list for scanability
- Remove unused import: directories::ProjectDirs (was from earlier
  config module experimentation, not needed until config implementation)

Let-chain pattern refactoring (storage/sqlite.rs):
Convert nested if-let in insert_conversation_tree to let-chain pattern
(Rust edition 2024 feature enabled via rust-toolchain.toml nightly):

Before:
```rust
if let Some(ext) = &conv.external_id {
    if let Some(existing): Option<i64> = tx.query_row(...).optional()? {
        return Ok(existing);
    }
}
```

After:
```rust
if let Some(ext) = &conv.external_id
    && let Some(existing) = tx.query_row(...).optional()?
{
    return Ok(existing);
}
```

Benefits of let-chain:
- Flat structure: eliminates nesting level, improves readability for
  multi-condition guards
- Short-circuit evaluation: if external_id is None, query_row never
  executes (avoids unnecessary database round-trip)
- Type inference: Option<i64> annotation removed, compiler infers from
  query_row return type and Ok(existing) usage
- Clippy recommended: matches modern Rust idioms, future-proof for
  edition 2024 stabilization

Import ordering (storage/sqlite.rs):
Alphabetize rusqlite imports (Connection, OptionalExtension, Transaction,
params) and anyhow imports (Context, Result, anyhow) per rustfmt default
rules. Reduces git diff noise when adding imports (new imports slot into
alphabetical position, don't cause full group reorder).

Formatting changes (connectors/codex.rs):
- Let-chain pattern in rollout_paths filter: entry.file_type().is_file()
  && entry.file_name().to_str().map(...).unwrap_or(false) flattened
  into single if condition (was nested if)
- .first() instead of .get(0): Clippy lint suggestion, more idiomatic
  for vec access (expresses "get first element if exists" intent vs
  "get element at arbitrary index 0")
- Long serde_json::from_str line split: rustfmt wraps at 100-char limit,
  assigns to temporary variable for readability

No functional changes:
All modifications are mechanical formatting/refactoring without behavior
changes:
- Let-chain conditions evaluate identically to nested if-let (same
  short-circuit semantics, same value extraction)
- Import reordering doesn't affect resolution (Rust uses absolute paths,
  order irrelevant to compiler)
- .first() vs .get(0) produces identical machine code (both inline to
  length check + unsafe pointer offset)

Validation:
Confirmed via:
- cargo check --all-targets: No new errors/warnings
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings: Clean (no lints triggered)
- cargo fmt --check: Passes (all files formatted per rustfmt.toml rules)
- Manual inspection: git diff shows only whitespace, import order, and
  let-chain pattern changes (no logic modifications)

Why commit separately from feature changes:
Isolating formatting in dedicated commit enables:
- Clean feature commit diffs: previous commits (Codex/Cline/Gemini
  connectors) show only new functionality, not mixed with formatting
- Easier code review: reviewer sees "formatting only" commit label,
  skips detailed review (trusts rustfmt/clippy), focuses on feature
  commits
- Bisect-friendly: if regression introduced, git bisect ignore this
  commit (no behavior change means can't introduce bug)
- Rebase-safe: formatting commits rarely conflict when rebasing (only
  touch whitespace/order, not semantics)

Future prevention:
Consider adding pre-commit hook running cargo fmt and cargo clippy to
catch formatting drift before commit (AGENTS.md line 61-75 emphasizes
running these commands manually, automation reduces friction).

Related: Cargo.toml (edition 2024 enabling let-chains), rust-toolchain.toml
(nightly for unstable features), AGENTS.md line 72-74 (cargo fmt/clippy
requirements).
